Document 2: Requires the head of a federal agency to approve in writing any conference costing more than $25,000
Publish a summary on the agency website of the conference that includes the purpose and total cost of the conference.
Document 3: Establishes the Federal Program Sunset Commission to review each activity or project listed in the program and financing schedules
Requires a federal agency to make the program performance report for each program under its control available to Congress upon request.
Document 4: Requires each executive agency to use plain language in any covered document issued or substantially revised
Agency heads to report on compliance to OMB.
Document 5: Requires the Office of Personnel Management to submit an annual report to Congress on the use of official time by federal employees
Defines 'official time' as any period of time granted to a federal employee to perform representational or consultative functions.
Document 6: Establishes the Senate Committee to Reduce Government Waste to research, review, and study federal programs that are underperforming or nonessential
Recommend to the Senate actions designed to modify or eliminate them.
Document 7: Expands the requirement for heads of executive agencies to identify major information technology acquisition programs
Requires each Chief Information Officer of a federal agency to identify its major IT investments that are most critical.
Document 8: Amends the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) to require federal agencies to make their disclosable records and documents available for public inspection
Establish a Chief FOIA Officers Council to develop recommendations for increasing compliance with FOIA requirements.
Document 9: Directs the Government Publishing Office to establish and maintain a public website containing Congressional Research Service Reports
The website shall include CRS Authorization of Appropriations Products, Appropriations Products, and any other written CRS product.
Document 10: Amends the Sex Offender Notification and Registration Act to require jurisdictions to prohibit registered sex offenders from accepting a position of employment with minors
Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to devise a strategy to reduce overall government printing costs.

topic.name: Universal Service and Broadband Access
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to telecommunications services and broadband for underserved populations and ensuring fair competition among service providers.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to direct the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to adopt a final rule establishing support under the Universal Service Fund Lifeline Assistance Program for qualifying low-income consumers
Requires the program to allow qualifying consumers to elect to apply support from the Lifeline program to basic telephone service, voice telephony service, or broadband Internet access service.
Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require a telecommunications carrier... that receives federal universal service support... to offer automatic roaming services
Prohibits construing this Act as prescribing particular rates for the provision of automatic roaming services.
Document 4: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 concerning procedures for the review of universal service requirements
Includes high-speed broadband services within universal service.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative amendments and reforms to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at simplifying tax processes, enhancing taxpayer rights, and addressing issues such as tax compliance and enforcement.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the subchapter S election for corporate taxpayers
extend the deadline for filing such election to the due date of the corporation's tax return
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to impose an Internet gambling license fee on Internet gambling operators
require such operators to file informational returns identifying themselves and the individuals placing wagers with them
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow businesses with average annual gross receipts of not more than $50,000 to recover their costs incurred in tax proceedings
increase the penalties against federal officers or employees for unlawful acts in connection with internal revenue laws
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require the Secretary of the Treasury to make certain disclosures to the holder of a social security account number
authorize the FBI Director to disclose information received from the Secretary to federal, state, and local law enforcement officials
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to authorize the Department of the Treasury to issue an individual taxpayer identification number
requires the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration to audit the IRS program for issuance of ITINs
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to implement a program to prevent the fraudulent use of taxpayer identification numbers
requires a comparison of all income tax returns filed with the United States and any U.S. territory
Document 7: Repeals the income tax, employment tax, and estate and gift tax
imposes a national sales tax on the use or consumption in the United States
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require the IRS to include in its acknowledgment of receipt of an application of a charitable organization a notice of the right of an organization to obtain a declaratory judgment
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require the Internal Revenue Service to mail paper income tax forms to taxpayers who file paper returns
Document 10: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to tax shelter activities to establish legal presumptions against the validity of transactions involving offshore secrecy jurisdictions
increases penalties for promoting abusive tax shelters and for aiding and abetting the understatement of tax liability

topic.name: Small Business Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative reforms and acts aimed at supporting and improving small business operations and access to funding in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to consider an individual with a net worth of less than $978,722 as eligible for participation in a Small Business Administration (SBA) program providing grants to small businesses owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals.
Requires such amount to be adjusted annually for inflation.
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to obtaining the best available collateral for a disaster loan of up to $200,000 relating to damage to or destruction of the property of, or economic injury to, a small business.
Prohibits the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) from requiring the small business owner to use the owner's primary residence as collateral.
Document 3: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish a dedicated process by which credit unions participating in a certain small business loan program may receive approvals and repurchases concerning program loan guarantees.
Amends the Small Business Act to direct the SBA Administrator to implement a program to provide outreach and assistance to credit unions.
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Act to increase the percentage amount that federal agencies must spend with small businesses participating in federal small business innovative research (SBIR) and small business technology transfer (STTR) programs.
Document 5: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish and implement an expedited disaster assistance business loan program to provide small businesses with immediate disaster assistance.
Document 6: Amends the Small Business Act to authorize a federal contract opportunity to be awarded on the basis of competition restricted to qualified HUBZone small businesses.
Prohibits a business from being included in a database maintained by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs until the Secretary verifies that the business is owned and controlled by veterans.
Document 7: Makes Brevard County, Florida, a HUBZone until no earlier than January 1, 2020.
Document 8: Authorizes the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to make grants to small businesses to commercialize research developed by such businesses using funds received under the second phase of the Small Business Innovation Research Program.
Terminates the grant program at the end of FY2011.
Document 9: Directs the Federal Acquisition Regulatory Council to amend the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) to revise certain federal acquisition practices and procedures.
Requires executive agency heads to use online reverse auction in the procurement of commercial items above the simplified acquisition threshold.
Document 10: Establishes a three-year small business intermediary lending pilot program under which the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) may make direct loans to eligible nonprofit lending intermediaries.
Authorizes the Administrator to make one percent, 20-year loans of up to $3 million to up to 20 lending intermediaries.

topic.name: Mining and Environmental Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing safety and environmental protection in the mining industry and related energy sectors.

Document 1: conduct a study to determine the economic and environmental risks of oil or hazardous liquids spills
evaluate the condition and structural integrity of the facilities
Document 2: require the Secretary of Labor to determine why an accident occurred and whether there were violations of law
make recommendations to avoid any recurrence
Document 3: repeal the $15 million limitation placed upon the total annual payment to certified states or Indian tribes in connection with abandoned mine reclamation
Document 4: require the Administrator of the EPA to determine whether drilling fluids and other wastes meet hazardous waste criteria
promulgate regulations regarding standards applicable to generators, transporters, and owners
Document 5: prohibit acceptance of new self-bonds for coal reclamation
establish limitations on surety bonds to minimize the financial liability
Document 6: expand OSHA coverage to federal, state, and local government employees
increases protections for whistle blowers under OSHA
Document 7: declares OSHA inapplicable to working conditions covered by the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act
prescribes requirements relating to employer's duty to furnish a place of employment free from recognized hazards
Document 8: prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing regulations that would adversely impact employment in coal mines
reduce the quantity of coal available for domestic consumption or export
Document 9: require the Secretary of Labor to issue citations and penalties in case of violations
make recommendations to avoid any recurrence
Document 10: require emergency response plans to provide for post communication systems
prohibits miners from being disciplined based on information obtained from an electronic communications and tracking system

topic.name: Unemployment Compensation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to the extension and improvement of unemployment compensation and assistance programs in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation (EUC) program through June 2, 2010.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the tax credit for the health insurance costs of trade adjustment assistance (TAA) workers.
Allows a permanent extension of COBRA coverage for certain TAA workers and PBGC recipients.
Document 3: Amends the Middle Class Tax Relief and Job Creation Act of 2012 with respect to state short-time compensation programs.
Extends federal financing of the programs for an additional year.
Document 6: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Extends the Emergency Unemployment Compensation (EUC) program through December 31, 2010.
Document 10: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Postpones the termination of the program until February 28, 2011.

topic.name: School Safety and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving safety, support, and educational resources in schools to address issues such as bullying, healthy relationships, student well-being, and equitable funding.

Document 1: require states to use grants for safe and drug-free schools to collect and report information on the incidence of bullying and harassment
establish grievance procedures for students and parents to register complaints regarding such conduct
Document 2: authorize the Department of Education to award competitive four-year grants to local educational agencies to provide professional development in safe relationship behavior education
includes education regarding healthy relationships and consent
Document 4: condition local educational agency receipt of school improvement funds on average state and local spending per pupil
requires annual LEA report cards to include certain information on state and local spending per pupil in schools
Document 7: promote safety and combat youth violence and substance abuse in public middle or secondary schools
employ a Parent Leader from among the parents of students to work on a full-time basis with students, other parents, and school officials
Document 10: requires local educational agencies to have in effect policies that deem to be reasonable and permissible a search of any minor student on public school grounds
denies Safe and Drug Free School funds to local educational agencies that fail to comply with this Act

topic.name: Federal Law Enforcement and Victim Assistance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing federal law enforcement capabilities, improving training, and providing assistance to victims of various crimes including child abduction, elder abuse, and sexual assault.

Document 1: International and Parental Child Abduction Remedies Assistance Act
provide funding, technical assistance, and training to legal providers to assist victims of parental kidnappings.
Document 2: Office of Elder Justice
address issues relating to elder abuse.
Document 8: Sexual Assault Forensic Evidence Reporting Act of 2013
ensure that the collection and processing of DNA evidence by law enforcement agencies from crimes is carried out in an appropriate and timely manner.

topic.name: Offshore Energy Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulations related to the exploration, development, and management of offshore energy resources, including oil, natural gas, and renewable energy projects.

Document 1: Amends the Mineral Leasing Act to direct the Secretary of the Interior to collect for each of FY2016-FY2026 a fee of $9,500 for each new application for a drilling permit.
Makes the Fund available to the Secretary for expenditure for the coordination and processing of oil and gas use authorizations on Indian trust mineral estate land.
Document 2: Permits United States persons to engage in exploration and extraction of hydrocarbon resources from any portion of any foreign exclusive economic zone contiguous to the exclusive economic zone of the United States.
Amends the Gulf of Mexico Energy Security Act of 2006 to repeal the moratorium on oil and gas leasing east of the Military Mission Line in the Gulf of Mexico.
Document 3: Prohibit the Department of the Interior from issuing or renewing a lease or any other authorization for the exploration, development, or production of oil, natural gas, or any other mineral in the Arctic Ocean.
Declares that it is the policy of the United States that the Arctic Ocean should be managed for the best interests of the people of the United States.
Document 4: Exempts any project determined by the Secretary of the Interior to be an offshore meteorological site testing and monitoring project from environmental impact statement requirements.
Defines an 'offshore meteorological site testing and monitoring project' as a project that is administered by the Department of the Interior.
Document 5: Deems the Draft Proposed Outer Continental Shelf Oil and Gas Leasing Program 2010-2015 issued by the Secretary of the Interior to be approved as a final oil and gas leasing program.
Directs the Secretary to conduct a lease sale in each outer Continental Shelf (OCS) planning area for which there is a commercial interest in purchasing federal oil and gas production leases.
Document 6: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to accept bids on any new oil and gas leases of federal lands only from bidders certifying that all crude oil produced under such leases shall be offered for sale only in the United States.
Authorizes the President to waive such limited leasing authorization upon specified determinations.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to hold a lease sale offering an additional 10 parcels for lease for research, development, and demonstration of oil shale resources.
Applies the oil shale management final rules published by the Department of the Interior on November 18, 2008, to all commercial leasing for the management of federally owned oil shale.
Document 8: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to repeal the research and development program for ultra-deepwater and unconventional onshore natural gas and other petroleum assets.
Document 9: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing any oil or gas lease sale under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act with royalty-based incentives in any tract located in water depths of 400 meters or more.
Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to repeal royalty relief for deep water oil and gas production.
Document 10: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit the Department of the Interior from issuing a lease for the exploration, development, or production of oil or natural gas in any area of the outer Continental Shelf off the coast of California, Oregon, or Washington.

topic.name: Waterway Restoration and Conservation
topic.description: Legislative measures focused on the restoration, protection, and management of various U.S. waterways and their ecosystems.

Document 1: Columbia Basin Restoration Program
collaborative toxic contamination reduction approach for the Columbia River Basin
Document 2: Delaware River Basin restoration program
identify, prioritize, and implement restoration and protection activities
Document 3: Chesapeake Bay watershed restoration activities
financial report on restoration activities in the Chesapeake Bay watershed
Document 5: nutria eradication program
restore nutria-damaged wetland
Document 9: Comprehensive Conservation and Management Plan for Long Island Sound
strategies to increase education and awareness about the ecological health and water quality
Document 10: Gulf Coast Restoration Trust Fund
coastal restoration projects and activities

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code related to tax exemptions, credits, and repeals affecting individuals and specific sectors.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow bonds guaranteed by any federal home loan bank to be treated as tax-exempt state or local bonds.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to advance the termination date of the income and excise tax credits for alcohol fuel mixtures (ethanol).
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income rebates of state or local real or personal property taxes.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the excise tax on employer-sponsored health care coverage for high-cost plans.
Document 5: This bill extends the tax credit for health insurance costs of a taxpayer and qualifying family members through 2019.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow taxpayers who received a mandatory distribution from their pension plan to recontribute such amounts without tax or penalty.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the tax credit for expenses for household and dependent care services necessary for gainful employment.
Document 8: Makes permanent provisions of the Jobs and Growth Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2003 that reduce income tax rates.
Document 9: Repeals provisions of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001 eliminating the estate tax.
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude distributions from retirement accounts used to pay long-term care insurance premiums.
